title: cicuta virosa var. tenuifolia
intro: Cicuta virosa var. tenuifolia, also known as a variant of the water hemlock, is a highly toxic and potent plant species. Despite its toxicity, it has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, primarily as a purgative and anthelmintic. The plant's roots have been used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and parasitic worm infections, due to its active compounds, including cicutoxin and cicutol. Traditionally, the roots of Cicuta virosa var. tenuifolia have been prepared as a tincture or infusions for medicinal purposes, but extreme caution and under the guidance of a medical professional are essential due to the plant's toxicity.
